name: Cappuccino Malt Float
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, heat the cream, half-and-half, 1/3 cup of granulated sugar, espresso, and cinnamon over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sugar is dissolved. Increase the heat to high and bring the mixture to a gentle boil. Place the cocoa powder in a small bowl. Add 2 to 3 tablespoons of the hot cream mixture to the cocoa and stir well to form a smooth paste. Return the cocoa powder mixture to the saucepan and whisk until smooth. Remove from heat, cover, and allow to infuse for 10 minutes.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk egg yolks, remaining 1/3 cup sugar, and salt until blended.
3. Strain hot cream mixture through cheesecloth-lined sieve into a medium, heavy saucepan. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil. Remove from heat. Gradually whisk about 1 cup of hot cream mixture into beaten egg yolks until blended. Return this mixture to the saucepan. Continue cooking over medium-low heat, stirring constantly with wooden spoon for 2 to 4 minutes, or until custard has thickened slightly. It is done when a path remains on spoon after cooling.
4. Remove from heat and strain through fine-mesh sieve into stainless bowl. Place bowl over larger bowl of ice water and stir the custard for 5 to 10 minutes, or until cool. Stir in vanilla. Remove from ice water. Cover surface with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 6 hours or overnight until very cold.
5. Scrape chilled custard into container of ice cream maker and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions. Transfer ice cream to medium bowl. Cover surface with plastic wrap and tightly cover bowl with aluminum foil. Freeze overnight.
1. In chilled 4 1/2-quart bowl, beat cream, sugar, and vanilla until forms slightly stiff peaks. Fill pastry bag fitted with star tip (such as Ateco #6) with whipped cream and refrigerate.
2. In blender, combine milk, malt powder, and chocolate syrup with 2 scoops cappuccino ice cream. Blend at high speed for 45 seconds, until smooth. Pour into tall glass.
3. Place scoop of cappuccino ice cream on side of glass. Pipe large rosette of whipped cream next to ice cream. Garnish malt float with chocolate shavings and chocolate espresso bean. Repeat assembly to make 3 more malt floats.
4 servings
Preparation: 1 hour plus chilling and freezing times
